cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ("OS Command Injection")

extra.message: Cryptographically weak pseudo-random number generator (random.sample) is used to generate session tokens. Use secrets.token_urlsafe() instead.

cwe: CWE-338: Use of Cryptographically Weak Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)

extra.message: No X-Frame-Options header is set and CSP lacks frame-ancestors directive, making the application vulnerable to clickjacking attacks.

cwe: CWE-1021: Improper Restriction of Rendered UI Layers or Frames

extra.message: X-XSS-Protection header is explicitly set to 0, disabling the browser's built-in reflected XSS filter.

cwe: CWE-16: Configuration

extra.message: Content-Security-Policy allows "default-src *" and "unsafe-inline", allowing scripts from any origin and inline scripts, completely neutralizing CSP-based XSS protection.

extra.message: User input from params["id"] is concatenated directly into a SQL query string passed to cursor.execute(), allowing SQL injection.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ("SQL Injection")

extra.message: Database content (column values) from the users table is rendered directly into HTML table cells without any HTML escaping, allowing stored XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ("Cross-site Scripting")

extra.message: User input from params["msg"] is assigned to content and returned directly to the browser without any HTML escaping, allowing reflected XSS.

extra.message: User input from params["path"] is directly injected into JavaScript window.location.replace(), enabling both open redirect and XSS attacks.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ("Open Redirect")

extra.message: Access control relies solely on a client-side JavaScript redirect instead of a server-side authorization check, allowing unauthenticated access to sensitive content.

cwe: CWE-862: Missing Authorization

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ("OS Command Injection")

extra.message: User input from params["object"] is base64-decoded and passed directly to pickle.loads() without any validation, allowing arbitrary code execution via malicious pickled objects.

cwe: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data

extra.message: Hardcoded credentials (username "dsvpwa" / password "dsvpwa") are embedded in the authentication logic, creating a backdoor for attackers.
